34	---
35	
36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Lean Audit Report
100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope
108	- files audited: <N>
109	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
110	
111	## Per-file checklist
112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
146	
147	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
148	- ...
149	
150	## Minor
151	
152	Low-impact observations.
153	
154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```
173	
174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175	
176	## Return value
177	
178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182	
183	## Reminders
184	
185	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.

113	
114	The constant-coefficient combinatorial core of standard-cover Čech acyclicity

123	coefficient** complex `Cᵖ = (Fin (p+1) → ι) → M` with alternating coface
124	differential — abstracted away from the localisation/geometry so the purely
125	combinatorial cancellation (`combHomotopy`) and the simplicial identity
126	(`combDifferential_comp`, `d² = 0`) are isolated and reusable.  The remaining
127	work to close `CechAcyclic.affine` is the **L1** categorical bridge identifying
128	the abstract `CechComplex` terms with the away-localisation modules `M_{s_σ}` and

136	namespace CombinatorialCech

138	variable {ι : Type*} {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] {n : ℕ}
139	
140	/-- Alternating coface (Čech) differential with constant coefficients in `M`:
141	`Cⁿ = (Fin n → ι) → M ⟶ Cⁿ⁺¹`, `(d t)(σ) = ∑ⱼ (-1)ʲ • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)`.
142	The argument `σ ∘ j.succAbove` is the `(n)`-tuple obtained from the `(n+1)`-tuple
143	`σ` by deleting the `j`-th index. -/
144	private def combDifferential (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M :=
145	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (n + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)
146	
147	/-- The contracting homotopy of the localised complex: prepend the fixed
148	(globally invertible after localising at `s_r`) index `r`.
149	`(h u)(τ) = u (Fin.cons r τ)`. -/
150	private def combHomotopy (r : ι) (u : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) : (Fin n → ι) → M :=
151	  fun τ => u (Fin.cons r τ)
152	
153	@[simp] private lemma combHomotopy_zero (r : ι) :
154	    combHomotopy (M := M) (n := n) r 0 = 0 := by
155	  funext τ; simp [combHomotopy]

157	/-- Composing `Fin.cons r` with the `(j+1)`-th coface map is `Fin.cons r` of the
158	`j`-th coface map: the bookkeeping identity behind the homotopy computation. -/
159	private lemma cons_comp_succAbove_succ (r : ι) (σ : Fin (n + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (n + 1)) :
160	    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove
161	      = Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove) := by
162	  funext l
163	  refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ l
164	  · simp
165	  · intro i; simp [Fin.succ_succAbove_succ]
166	
167	/-- **Contracting-homotopy identity** (planner L3; Stacks
168	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`):
169	`d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id` on `Cⁿ⁺¹`.  This is the alternating-sum cancellation: the
170	`j = 0` term of `h (d t)` is `t`, and the remaining terms cancel against `d (h t)`
171	in pairs of opposite sign. -/
172	private lemma combHomotopy_spec (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) :
173	    combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) + combHomotopy r (combDifferential t) = t := by
174	  funext σ
175	  simp only [combDifferential, combHomotopy, Pi.add_apply]
176	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun j : Fin (n + 2) =>
177	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ j.succAbove))]
178	  have h0 : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (n + 2)).succAbove = σ := by
179	    funext i; simp
180	  rw [h0]
181	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
182	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
183	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
184	    rw [cons_comp_succAbove_succ, pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]; abel), add_zero]
185	
186	/-- Every cocycle is a coboundary in positive degree: if `d t = 0` then
187	`t = d (h t)`.  This is the homological content of `combHomotopy_spec` and is the
188	half (`ker d ⊆ im d`) carrying the geometric input. -/
189	private lemma combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M)
190	    (ht : combDifferential t = 0) : combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) = t := by
191	  have h := combHomotopy_spec r t
192	  rw [ht, combHomotopy_zero, add_zero] at h
193	  exact h

195	/-- Sign-cancellation behind `d² = 0`: under the index swap
196	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` the alternating sign flips. -/
197	private lemma combSign_flip (j : Fin (n + 2)) (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
198	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ)) * ((-1) ^ (i : ℕ))
199	      = - (((-1 : ℤ) ^ ((j.succAbove i : Fin (n + 2)) : ℕ))
200	            * ((-1) ^ ((i.predAbove j : Fin (n + 1)) : ℕ))) := by
201	  rcases lt_or_ge (i.castSucc) j with h | h
202	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h,
203	        Fin.val_castSucc, Fin.val_pred]
204	    have hpos : 0 < (j : ℕ) := lt_of_le_of_lt (Nat.zero_le _) (by exact_mod_cast h)
205	    obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ : ∃ m, (j : ℕ) = m + 1 := ⟨(j : ℕ) - 1, by omega⟩
206	    rw [hm]; simp only [Nat.add_sub_cancel, pow_succ]; ring
207	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h,
208	        Fin.val_succ, Fin.coe_castPred]
209	    rw [pow_succ]; ring

211	/-- **`d² = 0`** for the constant-coefficient alternating Čech complex.  Proved by
212	the standard sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` on
213	the double sum: the swap fixes the underlying composite coface
214	(`Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove`), is an involution
215	(`Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove`, `Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove`), has no
216	fixed point (`Fin.succAbove_ne`), and flips the sign (`combSign_flip`). -/
217	private lemma combDifferential_comp (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) :
218	    combDifferential (combDifferential t) = 0 := by
219	  funext σ
220	  simp only [combDifferential, Pi.zero_apply, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
221	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (n + 2) × Fin (n + 1) =>
222	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) • t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))]
223	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
224	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
225	    have harg : (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
226	        = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
227	      funext k
228	      simp only [Function.comp_apply]
229	      rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
230	    simp only [harg]
231	    rw [← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
232	    simp
233	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
234	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
235	    intro hj
236	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
237	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
238	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
239	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
240	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _

248	private lemma combDifferential_exact (r : ι) (n : ℕ) :
249	    Function.Exact (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M))
250	      (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 3) → ι) → M)) := by
251	  intro x
252	  constructor
253	  · intro hx
254	    exact ⟨combHomotopy r x, combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle r x hx⟩
255	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
256	    exact combDifferential_comp y

260	The localised complex that `exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner **L2**) consumes

263	localising the whole extended complex at a spanning element `s_r`, the index `r`
264	is globally available and `s_r` becomes a unit, so the prepend
265	`σ ↦ Fin.cons r σ` is an *isomorphism* on the coefficients:
266	`M_{s_{cons r σ}} = M_{s_r · s_σ} ≅ M_{s_σ}` because `s_r` is already invertible.

314	private def depDiff {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) :
315	    ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ :=
316	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (m + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • δ m σ j (t (σ ∘ j.succAbove))
317	
318	/-- Dependent contracting homotopy: prepend the distinguished index `r`, then
319	apply the prepend map `c`. -/
320	private def depHomotopy {m : ℕ} (u : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ) :
321	    ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ :=
322	  fun σ => c m σ (u (Fin.cons r σ))
323	
324	/-- **Dependent contracting-homotopy identity** (planner L3 port): `d ∘ h + h ∘ d
325	= id`, evaluated at a tuple `σ`.  Same alternating-sum cancellation as
326	`combHomotopy_spec`, threaded through the varying coefficients via the unit
327	identity `hu` (`c ∘ δ₀ = id`) and the shift identity `hsh`
328	(`c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c`). -/
329	private lemma depHomotopy_spec
330	    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
331	        (y : A (m + 1)
332	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
333	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
334	          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
335	    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
336	        (y : A (m + 1)
337	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
338	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
339	          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
340	    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
341	    depDiff δ (depHomotopy r c t) σ + depHomotopy r c (depDiff δ t) σ = t σ := by
342	  simp only [depDiff, depHomotopy]
343	  rw [map_sum]
344	  simp only [map_zsmul]
345	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun k : Fin (m + 2) =>
346	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (k : ℕ) • c (m + 1) σ
347	      (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) k (t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ k.succAbove))))]
348	  -- the `k = 0` term collapses to `t σ` via the unit identity
349	  rw [hu σ (t _)]
350	  rw [depTransport (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) t]
351	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
352	  -- the `k = j.succ` terms cancel against the `d ∘ h` sum in pairs of opposite sign
353	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
354	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
355	    rw [hsh σ x (t _), depTransport (cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ x) t,
356	      pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]
357	    abel), add_zero]
358	
359	/-- Dependent cocycle⟹coboundary (the geometric half consumed by L2): if
360	`depDiff t = 0` then `t = depDiff (depHomotopy t)`. -/
361	private lemma depDiff_eq_of_cocycle
362	    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
363	        (y : A (m + 1)
364	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
365	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
366	          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
367	    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
368	        (y : A (m + 1)
369	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
370	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
371	          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
372	    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ)
373	    (ht : depDiff δ t = 0) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
374	    depDiff δ (depHomotopy r c t) σ = t σ := by
375	  have h := depHomotopy_spec r δ c hu hsh t σ
376	  rw [show depHomotopy r c (depDiff δ t) σ = 0 by rw [ht]; simp [depHomotopy], add_zero] at h
377	  exact h
378	
379	omit [∀ m σ, AddCommGroup (A m σ)] in
380	/-- The composite coface tuple is symmetric under the `d²=0` index swap
381	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` (dependent analogue of the `harg` step
382	inside `combDifferential_comp`). -/
383	private lemma comp_succAbove_swap {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι)
384	    (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1)) :
385	    (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
386	      = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
387	  funext k
388	  simp only [Function.comp_apply]
389	  rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
390	
391	/-- **Dependent `d² = 0`** for the varying-coefficient alternating Čech complex.
392	Same sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` as
393	`combDifferential_comp`, with the coefficient transport handled by
394	`comp_succAbove_swap` and the coface-commutation hypothesis `hcomm` (the two
395	restriction maps into `M_{s_σ}` agree — a formal property of localisation maps,
396	independent of the choice of `r`). -/
397	private lemma depDiff_comp
398	    (hcomm : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1))
399	        (z : A m ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove)),
400	        δ (m + 1) σ j (δ m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) i z)
401	          = δ (m + 1) σ (j.succAbove i)
402	              (δ m (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) (i.predAbove j)
403	                ((comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm ▸ z)))
404	    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) :
405	    depDiff δ (depDiff δ t) = 0 := by
406	  funext σ
407	  simp only [depDiff, Pi.zero_apply, map_sum, map_zsmul, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
408	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (m + 2) × Fin (m + 1) =>
409	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) •
410	      δ (m + 1) σ p.1 (δ m (σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) p.2 (t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))))]
411	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
412	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
413	    simp only []
414	    rw [hcomm σ j i (t _), depTransport (comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm t,
415	      ← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
416	    simp
417	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
418	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
419	    intro hj
420	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
421	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
422	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
423	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
424	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _
425	
426	/-- **Dependent positive-degree exactness** in the `Function.Exact` form that
427	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner **L2**) consumes after localising at a
428	spanning element `s_r`.  Combines `depDiff_comp` (`im ⊆ ker`) with
429	`depDiff_eq_of_cocycle` (`ker ⊆ im`, the homotopy half carrying the
430	`s_r`-invertibility input).  This is the dependent-coefficient analogue of
431	`combDifferential_exact`. -/
432	private lemma depDiff_exact
433	    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
434	        (y : A (m + 1)
435	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
436	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
437	          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
438	    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
439	        (y : A (m + 1)
440	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
441	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
442	          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
443	    (hcomm : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1))
444	        (z : A m ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove)),
445	        δ (m + 1) σ j (δ m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) i z)
446	          = δ (m + 1) σ (j.succAbove i)
447	              (δ m (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) (i.predAbove j)
448	                ((comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm ▸ z)))
449	    (m : ℕ) :
450	    Function.Exact (depDiff δ (m := m + 1)) (depDiff δ (m := m + 2)) := by
451	  intro x
452	  constructor
453	  · intro hx
454	    exact ⟨depHomotopy r c x,
455	      funext fun σ => depDiff_eq_of_cocycle r δ c hu hsh x hx σ⟩
456	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
457	    exact depDiff_comp δ hcomm y
458	
459	end Dependent
460	
461	end CombinatorialCech
462	
463	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — away-localisation comparison maps
464	
465	The concrete `δ` (coface) and `c` (prepend) maps that the dependent combinatorial
466	core `CombinatorialCech.Dependent.depDiff_exact` consumes are, for the standard
467	affine cover, the canonical comparison maps between away-localisations
468	`M_a → M_b` available whenever `a ∣ b` — so that `a`, a divisor of the invertible
469	`b`, is itself invertible in `M_b`.  This section builds that comparison map and
470	its functoriality from `IsLocalizedModule` abstractly, independent of the sheaf
471	identification (L1), so it can be assembled into the localised {\v C}ech complex.
472	
473	Everything here is `R`-module algebra; no sheaf theory enters. The maps are
474	characterised by uniqueness of localisation lifts (`IsLocalizedModule.lift_unique`),
475	which is what makes the composition/identity laws — the algebraic heart of the
476	`hu`/`hsh`/`hcomm` compatibilities — provable. -/
477	
478	namespace AwayComparison
479	
480	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
481	
482	/-- The clean hypothesis under which a canonical comparison `M_a → Mb` exists: the
483	element `a` acts invertibly on the target localisation `Mb`. Because the scalar
484	action is `algebraMap` into the (central) endomorphism ring, this is closed under
485	multiplication and holds for any divisor of an already-invertible element — exactly
486	the two ways `δ` (divisibility) and `c` (a product of invertible factors) arise. -/
487	def Inverts (a : R) (Mb : Type u) [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb] : Prop :=
488	  IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R Mb) a)
489	
490	/-- A divisor `a` of `b` acts invertibly on any localisation `M_b` of `M` away
491	from `b`: since `b` is a unit there and `b = a · c` with the scalar actions
492	commuting (both lie in the image of the central `algebraMap`), `a` is a unit too. -/
493	lemma Inverts.of_dvd {a b : R} (hab : a ∣ b)
494	    {Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb] (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb)
495	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b) fb] :
496	    Inverts a Mb := by
497	  obtain ⟨c, rfl⟩ := hab
498	  have hb : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R Mb) (a * c)) :=
499	    IsLocalizedModule.map_units fb ⟨a * c, Submonoid.mem_powers _⟩
500	  rw [map_mul] at hb
501	  have hcomm : Commute (algebraMap R (Module.End R Mb) a) (algebraMap R (Module.End R Mb) c) :=
502	    (Commute.all a c).map _
503	  exact (hcomm.isUnit_mul_iff.mp hb).1
504	
505	/-- `Inverts` is closed under multiplication: a product of invertibly-acting
506	elements acts invertibly. This supplies the `c` (prepend) comparison, whose source
507	localising element `s_r · s_{cons r σ}` factors into divisors of the target. -/
508	lemma Inverts.mul {a a' : R} {Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
509	    (ha : Inverts a Mb) (ha' : Inverts a' Mb) : Inverts (a * a') Mb := by
510	  rw [Inverts, map_mul]
511	  exact IsUnit.mul ha ha'
512	
513	/-- The power version of the `Inverts` hypothesis, as the `IsLocalizedModule.lift`
514	universal property consumes it (`∀ x ∈ powers a`). -/
515	lemma Inverts.isUnit_powers {a : R} {Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
516	    (ha : Inverts a Mb) (x : Submonoid.powers a) :
517	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R Mb) (x : R)) := by
518	  obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := x.2
519	  rw [← hn, map_pow]
520	  exact ha.pow n
521	
522	/-- The canonical comparison map `M_a → Mb` between localisations of `M`, defined
523	whenever `a` acts invertibly on `Mb` (`Inverts a Mb`). It is the unique `R`-linear
524	map `M_a → Mb` commuting with the localisation structure maps (`comparison_unique`);
525	this universal characterisation is what makes the functoriality laws below provable. -/
526	noncomputable def comparison {a : R}
527	    {Ma Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
528	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
529	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) (hb : Inverts a Mb) :
530	    Ma →ₗ[R] Mb :=
531	  IsLocalizedModule.lift (Submonoid.powers a) fa fb hb.isUnit_powers
532	
533	@[simp] lemma comparison_apply {a : R}
534	    {Ma Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
535	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
536	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) (hb : Inverts a Mb) (x : M) :
537	    comparison fa fb hb (fa x) = fb x :=
538	  IsLocalizedModule.lift_apply _ _ _ _ x
539	
540	lemma comparison_comp_structure {a : R}
541	    {Ma Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
542	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
543	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) (hb : Inverts a Mb) :
544	    comparison fa fb hb ∘ₗ fa = fb :=
545	  IsLocalizedModule.lift_comp _ _ _ _
546	
547	/-- Uniqueness: any `R`-linear `l : M_a → Mb` commuting with the structure maps
548	equals the comparison map. -/
549	lemma comparison_unique {a : R}
550	    {Ma Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
551	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
552	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) (hb : Inverts a Mb)
553	    (l : Ma →ₗ[R] Mb) (hl : l ∘ₗ fa = fb) :
554	    comparison fa fb hb = l :=
555	  IsLocalizedModule.lift_unique _ _ _ _ l hl
556	
557	/-- **Identity law.** The comparison of a localisation with itself is the identity. -/
558	@[simp] lemma comparison_self {a : R}
559	    {Ma : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma]
560	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa] (ha : Inverts a Ma) :
561	    comparison fa fa ha = LinearMap.id :=
562	  comparison_unique _ _ _ _ (by ext x; simp)
563	
564	/-- **Composition law** (functoriality): comparison maps compose.  Proved by
565	uniqueness — both sides become `fc` after precomposing with the structure map `fa`.
566	This is the reusable algebraic core underlying the `hsh`/`hcomm` compatibilities of
567	the dependent {\v C}ech port. -/
568	lemma comparison_comp {a b : R}
569	    {Ma Mb Mc : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
570	    [AddCommGroup Mc] [Module R Mc]
571	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
572	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b) fb]
573	    (fc : M →ₗ[R] Mc)
574	    (hb : Inverts a Mb) (hc : Inverts b Mc) (hac : Inverts a Mc) :
575	    (comparison fb fc hc) ∘ₗ (comparison fa fb hb) = comparison fa fc hac :=
576	  (comparison_unique fa fc hac _ (by
577	    ext x
578	    simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, comparison_apply])).symm
579	
580	/-- Pointwise form of the composition law. -/
581	lemma comparison_comp_apply {a b : R}
582	    {Ma Mb Mc : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
583	    [AddCommGroup Mc] [Module R Mc]
584	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
585	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b) fb]
586	    (fc : M →ₗ[R] Mc)
587	    (hb : Inverts a Mb) (hc : Inverts b Mc) (hac : Inverts a Mc) (x : Ma) :
588	    comparison fb fc hc (comparison fa fb hb x) = comparison fa fc hac x :=
589	  LinearMap.congr_fun (comparison_comp fa fb fc hb hc hac) x
590	
591	/-- A power of an invertibly-acting element acts injectively on a localisation:
592	the scalar-cancellation device behind localisation transitivity. -/
593	lemma Inverts.smul_pow_cancel {a : R} {N : Type u} [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
594	    (ha : Inverts a N) (n : ℕ) {p q : N} (h : a ^ n • p = a ^ n • q) : p = q := by
595	  have hu : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R N) (a ^ n)) := by
596	    rw [map_pow]; exact ha.pow n
597	  apply ((Module.End.isUnit_iff _).mp hu).injective
598	  simpa only [Module.algebraMap_end_eq_smul_id, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.id_coe,
599	    id_eq] using h
600	
601	/-- **Localisation transitivity for the away comparison.** If `Ma` localises `M`
602	away from `a` and `Mb` away from `a * b`, the comparison map `Ma → Mb` exhibits
603	`Mb` as the localisation of `Ma` away from `b` — the "localisation of a
604	localisation" identity `M_a[1/b] = M_{ab}`.  This is the keystone that feeds the
605	per-spanning-element reduction `exact_of_isLocalized_span` of the section Čech
606	complex: localising the un-localised coefficient `M_{s_σ}` at a spanning element
607	`s_r` yields the doubly-localised coefficient `M_{s_r · s_σ}` of `cechCoeff`. -/
608	lemma comparison_isLocalizedModule {a b : R}
609	    {Ma Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
610	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
611	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (a * b)) fb]
612	    (hb : Inverts a Mb) :
613	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b) (comparison fa fb hb) := by
614	  have hbB : Inverts b Mb := Inverts.of_dvd ⟨a, by rw [mul_comm]⟩ fb
615	  have haA : Inverts a Ma := Inverts.of_dvd dvd_rfl fa
616	  refine ⟨fun x => hbB.isUnit_powers x, ?_, ?_⟩
617	  · -- surjectivity
618	    intro y
619	    obtain ⟨⟨m, u⟩, hu⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers (a * b)) fb y
620	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := u.2
621	    have hnR : (a * b) ^ n = (u : R) := hn
622	    refine ⟨⟨IsLocalizedModule.mk' fa m (⟨a ^ n, n, rfl⟩ : Submonoid.powers a),
623	      (⟨b ^ n, n, rfl⟩ : Submonoid.powers b)⟩, ?_⟩
624	    apply hb.smul_pow_cancel n
625	    -- RHS: a^n • comparison (mk' fa m ⟨a^n⟩) = fb m
626	    have hz : (a ^ n : R) • IsLocalizedModule.mk' fa m (⟨a ^ n, n, rfl⟩ : Submonoid.powers a)
627	        = fa m := by
628	      have h := IsLocalizedModule.mk'_cancel' fa m (⟨a ^ n, n, rfl⟩ : Submonoid.powers a)
629	      rwa [Submonoid.smul_def] at h
630	    rw [Submonoid.smul_def, ← _root_.map_smul, hz, comparison_apply]
631	    -- LHS: a^n • (b^n • y) = fb m
632	    rw [smul_smul, ← mul_pow, hnR, ← Submonoid.smul_def]
633	    exact hu
634	  · -- exists_of_eq
635	    intro x₁ x₂ e
636	    set w := x₁ - x₂ with hw
637	    have hcw : comparison fa fb hb w = 0 := by rw [hw, map_sub, e, sub_self]
638	    obtain ⟨⟨m, s⟩, hs⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers a) fa w
639	    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := s.2
640	    have hsw : a ^ k • w = fa m := by
641	      rw [Submonoid.smul_def] at hs
642	      rwa [show (s : R) = a ^ k from hk.symm] at hs
643	    have hfbm : fb m = 0 := by
644	      have h1 : comparison fa fb hb (fa m) = 0 := by
645	        rw [← hsw, _root_.map_smul, hcw, smul_zero]
646	      rwa [comparison_apply] at h1
647	    have hmz : fb m = fb 0 := by rw [hfbm, map_zero]
648	    obtain ⟨d, hd⟩ := (IsLocalizedModule.eq_iff_exists (Submonoid.powers (a * b)) fb).mp hmz
649	    obtain ⟨j, hj⟩ := d.2
650	    have hdm : (a * b) ^ j • m = 0 := by
651	      rw [smul_zero] at hd
652	      rw [Submonoid.smul_def] at hd
653	      rwa [show (d : R) = (a * b) ^ j from hj.symm] at hd
654	    -- a^{j+k} • (b^j • w) = 0
655	    have key : (a * b) ^ j • (a ^ k • w) = (0 : Ma) := by
656	      rw [hsw, ← _root_.map_smul, hdm, map_zero]
657	    have hzero : a ^ (j + k) • (b ^ j • w) = (0 : Ma) := by
658	      rw [smul_smul] at key
659	      rw [smul_smul, show a ^ (j + k) * b ^ j = (a * b) ^ j * a ^ k by
660	        rw [mul_pow, pow_add]; ring]
661	      exact key
662	    have hbw : b ^ j • w = 0 :=
663	      haA.smul_pow_cancel (j + k) (hzero.trans (smul_zero _).symm)
664	    refine ⟨(⟨b ^ j, j, rfl⟩ : Submonoid.powers b), ?_⟩
665	    rw [Submonoid.smul_def, Submonoid.smul_def]
666	    rw [hw, smul_sub, sub_eq_zero] at hbw
667	    exact hbw
668	
669	end AwayComparison
670	
671	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — multi-index localising elements
672	
673	The localising element attached to a {\v C}ech multi-index `σ : Fin m → ι` is the
674	product `s_σ = ∏ₖ s (σ k)`.  The two divisibility facts below are exactly the
675	hypotheses the away-comparison maps of `AwayComparison` need to become the
676	coface `δ` and prepend `c` maps of the dependent combinatorial core: a coface
677	deletes an index (so `s_{σ∘dⱼ} ∣ s_σ`), and the prepend multiplies by `s r`. -/
678	
679	namespace CechLocalized
680	
681	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} (s : ι → R)
682	
683	/-- `s_σ = ∏ₖ s (σ k)`, the localising element for the multi-index `σ`. -/
684	def sprod {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) : R := ∏ k, s (σ k)
685	
686	@[simp] lemma sprod_cons {m : ℕ} (i : ι) (σ : Fin m → ι) :
687	    sprod s (Fin.cons i σ) = s i * sprod s σ := by
688	  simp [sprod, Fin.prod_univ_succ]
689	
690	/-- A coface (index deletion) only drops the factor `s (σ j)`, so the smaller
691	product divides the larger: `s_{σ∘dⱼ} ∣ s_σ`.  This supplies the `Inverts`
692	hypothesis for the coface comparison `δ`. -/
693	lemma sprod_succAbove_dvd {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 1)) :
694	    sprod s (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∣ sprod s σ := by
695	  have h : sprod s σ = s (σ j) * sprod s (σ ∘ j.succAbove) := by
696	    rw [sprod, sprod, Fin.prod_univ_succAbove _ j]; rfl
697	  exact ⟨s (σ j), by rw [h]; ring⟩
698	
699	/-! ### The localised coefficient family and the concrete `δ`/`c` maps
700	
701	After localising the whole section {\v C}ech complex at a spanning element `s r`,
702	its degree-`p` coefficient at the multi-index `σ` is `M` localised at the product
703	`s r · s_σ` (the away localisation at `s_σ`, further localised at `s r`).  We model
704	that double localisation by the single away localisation at `s r · s_σ`.  The coface
705	`δ` and prepend `c` maps are the away-comparison maps of `AwayComparison`; the
706	`Inverts` hypotheses come from `sprod_succAbove_dvd` (for `δ`) and from
707	`Inverts.mul` together with `sprod_cons` (for `c`). -/
708	
709	variable (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] (r : ι)
710	
711	/-- The localised {\v C}ech coefficient `A_σ = M_{s r · s_σ}` (after localising at the
712	fixed index `r`). -/
713	abbrev cechCoeff {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) : Type u :=
714	  LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M
715	
716	/-- The coface comparison `δ : A_{σ∘dⱼ} → A_σ`, the canonical localisation map for the
717	divisibility `s r · s_{σ∘dⱼ} ∣ s r · s_σ`. -/
718	noncomputable def cechCoface (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 1)) :
719	    cechCoeff s M r (σ ∘ j.succAbove) →+ cechCoeff s M r σ :=
720	  (AwayComparison.comparison (M := M)
721	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s (σ ∘ j.succAbove))) M)
722	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M)
723	    (AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd (mul_dvd_mul_left (s r) (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ j))
724	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M))).toAddMonoidHom
725	
726	/-- The prepend comparison `c : A_{cons r σ} → A_σ`, the canonical localisation map.
727	Its source localises at `s r · s_{cons r σ} = s r · (s r · s_σ)`, a product whose
728	factors are all invertible in `A_σ`, supplied by `Inverts.mul`. -/
729	noncomputable def cechPrepend (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin m → ι) :
730	    cechCoeff s M r (Fin.cons r σ) →+ cechCoeff s M r σ :=
731	  (AwayComparison.comparison (M := M)
732	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s (Fin.cons r σ))) M)
733	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M)
734	    (by
735	      change AwayComparison.Inverts (s r * sprod s (Fin.cons r σ))
736	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M)
737	      have h : s r * sprod s (Fin.cons r σ) = s r * (s r * sprod s σ) := by
738	        rw [sprod_cons]
739	      rw [h]
740	      exact AwayComparison.Inverts.mul
741	        (AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd ⟨sprod s σ, rfl⟩
742	          (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M))
743	        (AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd dvd_rfl
744	          (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M)))).toAddMonoidHom
745	
746	/-- A transport of a {\v C}ech coefficient along an equality of multi-index tuples
747	`τ₁ = τ₂` is the canonical comparison map (both localise `M` at the *same* element
748	`s r · s_τ`, since `s_τ` depends only on the function `τ`). This is the bridge
749	between the dependent-type transports `▸` of the combinatorial core and the
750	away-comparison maps. -/
751	lemma cechCoeff_transport_eq_comparison {m : ℕ} {τ₁ τ₂ : Fin m → ι} (hτ : τ₁ = τ₂)
752	    (hinv : AwayComparison.Inverts (s r * sprod s τ₁) (cechCoeff s M r τ₂))
753	    (y : cechCoeff s M r τ₁) :
754	    hτ ▸ y = AwayComparison.comparison
755	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s τ₁)) M)
756	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s τ₂)) M) hinv y := by
757	  subst hτ
758	  rw [AwayComparison.comparison_self]
759	  rfl
760	
761	/-- **Unit compatibility** `hu` for the concrete localised {\v C}ech maps:
762	`c ∘ δ₀ = transport`.  Deleting the prepended `r` (the `0`-th coface) and then
763	applying the prepend `c` is, on the away localisations, the identity transport,
764	because all three maps are away-comparison maps and compose by `comparison_comp`. -/
765	lemma cech_hu {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
766	    (y : cechCoeff s M r ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)) :
767	    cechPrepend s M r (m + 1) σ (cechCoface s M r (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
768	      = (CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y := by
769	  have heq : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove = σ :=
770	    CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ
771	  have hinv : AwayComparison.Inverts
772	      (s r * sprod s ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove))
773	      (cechCoeff s M r σ) :=
774	    AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd (dvd_of_eq (by rw [heq])) (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ M)
775	  simp only [cechPrepend, cechCoface, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe]
776	  change _ = heq ▸ y
777	  rw [cechCoeff_transport_eq_comparison s M r heq hinv,
778	    AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply]
779	
780	/-- **Shift compatibility** `hsh` for the concrete localised {\v C}ech maps:
781	`c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c` (modulo the index transport).  Both composites are
782	away-comparison maps from the same source localisation to the same target, hence
783	equal — assembled from `comparison_comp` and the transport bridge. -/
784	lemma cech_hsh {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
785	    (y : cechCoeff s M r ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)) :
786	    cechPrepend s M r (m + 1) σ (cechCoface s M r (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) k.succ y)
787	      = cechCoface s M r m σ k
788	          (cechPrepend s M r m (σ ∘ k.succAbove)
789	            ((CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)) := by
790	  have heq' : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove
791	      = Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove) := CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k
792	  have hinv' : AwayComparison.Inverts
793	      (s r * sprod s ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove))
794	      (cechCoeff s M r (Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove))) :=
795	    AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd (dvd_of_eq (by rw [heq'])) (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ M)
796	  have key : AwayComparison.Inverts (s r * sprod s (Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove)))
797	      (cechCoeff s M r σ) := by
798	    rw [show s r * sprod s (Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove))
799	          = s r * (s r * sprod s (σ ∘ k.succAbove)) from by rw [sprod_cons]]
800	    exact AwayComparison.Inverts.mul
801	      (AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd (dvd_mul_right (s r) (sprod s σ))
802	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ M))
803	      (AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd (mul_dvd_mul_left (s r) (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ k))
804	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ M))
805	  simp only [cechPrepend, cechCoface, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe]
806	  rw [cechCoeff_transport_eq_comparison s M r heq' hinv',
807	    AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply, AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply,
808	    AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply]
809	  · rw [heq']; exact key
810	  · exact key
811	
812	/-- **Coface commutation** `hcomm` (the `d² = 0` swap identity) for the concrete
813	localised {\v C}ech maps.  Both bracketings of the double coface are away-comparison
814	maps from the (swap-invariant) double-deletion localisation to `A_σ`, hence equal. -/
815	lemma cech_hcomm {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1))
816	    (z : cechCoeff s M r ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove)) :
817	    cechCoface s M r (m + 1) σ j (cechCoface s M r m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) i z)
818	      = cechCoface s M r (m + 1) σ (j.succAbove i)
819	          (cechCoface s M r m (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) (i.predAbove j)
820	            ((CombinatorialCech.comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm ▸ z)) := by
821	  have heqc : (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove
822	      = (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove :=
823	    (CombinatorialCech.comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm
824	  have hinvc : AwayComparison.Inverts (s r * sprod s ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove))
825	      (cechCoeff s M r ((σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove)) :=
826	    AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd (dvd_of_eq (by rw [heqc])) (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ M)
827	  have key : AwayComparison.Inverts (s r * sprod s ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove))
828	      (cechCoeff s M r σ) :=
829	    AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd
830	      (mul_dvd_mul_left (s r)
831	        (dvd_trans (sprod_succAbove_dvd s (σ ∘ j.succAbove) i) (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ j)))
832	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ M)
833	  simp only [cechCoface, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe]
834	  rw [cechCoeff_transport_eq_comparison s M r heqc hinvc,
835	    AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply, AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply,
836	    AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply]
837	  · exact key
838	  · rw [CombinatorialCech.comp_succAbove_swap]; exact key
839	
840	/-- **Positive-degree exactness of the localised section {\v C}ech complex.**
841	After localising the standard-cover section {\v C}ech complex at a spanning element
842	`s r`, the resulting complex of away-localisations `A_σ = M_{s r · s_σ}` is exact in
843	positive degrees.  This is the dependent combinatorial core
844	`CombinatorialCech.depDiff_exact` fed the concrete coface `cechCoface`, prepend
845	`cechPrepend`, and the compatibilities `cech_hu`/`cech_hsh`/`cech_hcomm`.  It is the
846	`Function.Exact` input that `exact_of_isLocalized_span` consumes node by node in the
847	L1 categorical→module bridge (the remaining gaps: the finite-product localisation
848	`∏_σ M_{s_σ}` and the sheaf-section identification `F(D(s_σ)) = M_{s_σ}`). -/
849	lemma cechLocalized_exact (m : ℕ) :
850	    Function.Exact
851	      (CombinatorialCech.depDiff (A := fun _ σ => cechCoeff s M r σ) (cechCoface s M r)
852	        (m := m + 1))
853	      (CombinatorialCech.depDiff (A := fun _ σ => cechCoeff s M r σ) (cechCoface s M r)
854	        (m := m + 2)) :=
855	  CombinatorialCech.depDiff_exact (A := fun _ σ => cechCoeff s M r σ) r
856	    (cechCoface s M r) (cechPrepend s M r)
857	    (cech_hu s M r) (cech_hsh s M r) (cech_hcomm s M r) m
858	
859	end CechLocalized
860	
861	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the un-localised section Čech module complex `D•`
862	
863	`D^m` is the product `∏_{σ : Fin m → ι} M_{s_σ}` of away-localisations of
864	`M = Γ(Spec R, F)` at the multi-index products `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}`; its differential
865	is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation comparison maps (index deletion
866	drops a factor, so `s_{σ∘dⱼ} ∣ s_σ`).  This is the concrete `R`-module complex `D•`
867	of `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` that the section Čech complex is identified
868	with.  Positive-degree exactness is reduced — via `exact_of_isLocalized_span` — to
869	the localised complexes already shown exact in `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`;
870	the bridge is the localisation-transitivity lemma
871	`AwayComparison.comparison_isLocalizedModule` (applied here as
872	`dToCech_isLocalizedModule`). -/
873	
874	namespace SectionCechModule
875	
876	open CechLocalized AwayComparison
877	
878	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} (s : ι → R)
879	variable (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
880	
881	/-- The un-localised section Čech coefficient `D_σ = M_{s_σ}`. -/
882	abbrev dCoeff {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) : Type u :=
883	  LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M
884	
885	/-- The un-localised coface comparison `δ : M_{s_{σ∘dⱼ}} → M_{s_σ}` (R-linear), the
886	canonical localisation map for the divisibility `s_{σ∘dⱼ} ∣ s_σ`. -/
887	noncomputable def dCoface (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 1)) :
888	    dCoeff s M (σ ∘ j.succAbove) →ₗ[R] dCoeff s M σ :=
889	  comparison
890	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s (σ ∘ j.succAbove))) M)
891	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M)
892	    (Inverts.of_dvd (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ j)
893	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M))
894	
895	/-- The un-localised differential `d : D^m → D^{m+1}` as an `R`-linear map: the
896	alternating sum of cofaces, assembled with `LinearMap.pi` over the output tuple. -/
897	noncomputable def dDiff (m : ℕ) :
898	    (∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) →ₗ[R] (∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, dCoeff s M σ) :=
899	  LinearMap.pi fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (m + 1),
900	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • (dCoface s M m σ j ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj (σ ∘ j.succAbove))
901	
902	/-- Apply form of `dDiff`: the alternating sum of cofaces of the deleted faces. -/
903	lemma dDiff_apply (m : ℕ) (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
904	    dDiff s M m t σ
905	      = ∑ j : Fin (m + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • dCoface s M m σ j (t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)) := by
906	  rw [dDiff, LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.sum_apply]
907	  exact Finset.sum_congr rfl fun d _ => rfl
908	
909	variable (r : ι)
910	
911	/-- The per-index localisation comparison `M_{s_σ} → M_{s_r · s_σ}` carrying the
912	un-localised coefficient to the `cechCoeff` coefficient localised at `s_r`. -/
913	noncomputable def dToCech {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) :
914	    dCoeff s M σ →ₗ[R] cechCoeff s M r σ :=
915	  comparison
916	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M)
917	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M)
918	    (Inverts.of_dvd (dvd_mul_left (sprod s σ) (s r))

14	                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplex}`)

16	                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomEquiv}`)

18	                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv}`)

23	- `cechFreePresheafComplex` / `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` → `FreePresheafComplex.lean`

27	universe u

31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry

33	/-
34	Planner strategy (P3b presheaf-Čech bridge; see analogies/p3b-presheafcech.md +
35	blueprint §Presheaf-level Čech machinery):
36	
37	The goal is to prove that injective `O_X`-modules have trivial positive-degree Čech
38	cohomology with respect to any open cover.  The strategy avoids `presheafModules_
39	enoughInjectives` and `cech_delta_functor_presheaves` (decisions 4–5 in the analogy
40	file are expensive Mathlib gaps and are NOT on the critical path).  The build order is:
41	
42	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43	Step 1 — `sectionCechComplex`
44	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
45	Type: given a scheme `X`, an open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover`, an open `U : Opens ↥X`,
46	and a presheaf of modules `F : X.PresheafOfModules`, produce the section Čech
47	cochain complex
48	
49	    Č•(𝒰, F, U) : CochainComplex (ModuleCat (X.ringCatSheaf.val.obj (op U))) ℕ
50	
51	whose degree-`p` term is the product `∏_{(i₀,…,iₚ) : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.J}
52	  F.obj (op (𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓ 𝒰.U iₚ ⊓ U))`, equipped with the alternating restriction
53	differential.
54	
55	THIS OBJECT IS DISTINCT from `CechComplex` in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean`:
56	`CechComplex` lives in `S.Modules` (pushforward along `f`) and is a relative complex.
57	`sectionCechComplex` is a plain cochain complex of `R`-modules where `R = O_X(U)`.
58	
59	Key Mathlib hooks:
60	- `PresheafOfModules` — the category `X.PresheafOfModules =
61	  PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.val`.
62	- `PresheafOfModules.evaluation R (op V) : PresheafOfModules R ⥤ ModuleCat (R.obj (op V))`
63	  — the exact "sections over `V`" functor; use it to extract `F.obj (op V)`.
64	- `CochainComplex.of` or `HomologicalComplex.mk` — assemble the complex from
65	  degree-`p` objects and differentials.
66	
67	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
68	Step 2 — `cechFreePresheafComplex`
69	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
70	Type: given `X` and `𝒰`, produce the chain complex of presheaves of modules
71	
72	    K(𝒰)_• : ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ
73	
74	whose degree-`p` term is the direct sum `⨁_{(i₀,…,iₚ) : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.J}
75	  (PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj (𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓ 𝒰.U iₚ))`.
76	
77	DO NOT introduce a bespoke extension-by-zero functor `j_!`.  The blueprint's
78	`(j_{i₀…iₚ})_! (O_X|_{U_{i₀…iₚ}})` is canonically identified with
79	`(PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj U_…)`: on sections over `V`, this is
80	the free `R(V)`-module on `Hom(V, U_…)` — which is `R(V)` when `V ⊆ U_…` and `0`
81	otherwise, matching extension-by-zero of `O_X|_{U_…}` exactly.
82	
83	The differentials are `(PresheafOfModules.free _).map` of the representable maps
84	`yoneda.obj (U_{i₀⊓…⊓U_{iₚ₊₁}}) → yoneda.obj (U_{i₀⊓…⊓̂ᵢⱼ⊓…⊓U_{iₚ₊₁}})` induced
85	by the inclusion of opens `𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓ 𝒰.U iₚ₊₁ ≤ 𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓̂ᵢⱼ ⊓ …`, with
86	the standard alternating sign.

88	Key Mathlib hooks:

295	cosimplicial map `f : [m] ⟶ [n]` acts at multi-index `σ : Fin (n+1) → ι` by projecting to
296	the coordinate `σ ∘ f` and then restricting along `⨅ₖ U (σ k) ≤ ⨅ₗ U (σ (f l))`. This is the
297	covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain

304	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
305	  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
306	  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
307	    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
308	      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
309	  map_id n := by
310	    apply Pi.hom_ext
311	    intro σ
312	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom,
313	      OrderHom.id_coe, Function.comp_id]
314	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)]
315	    simp
316	  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
317	    apply Pi.hom_ext
318	    intro σ
319	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
320	    rw [← Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
321	    congr 1

336	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)
